meaning: n.[希神]恩克拉多斯(反叛众神的巨人)
examples: Enceladus is Wired Science's favorite Saturnian moon.
Other moons affected by tidal forces are Enceladus (Saturn) and Triton (Neptune).
The view was acquired at a Sun-Enceladus-spacecraft, or phase, angle of 73 degrees.
The data from the Cassini spacecraft is said to strengthen the idea that Enceladus harbours a sub-surface sea.
Europa, a satellite of Jupiter, and Enceladus, which orbits Saturn, both have large volumes of liquid water buried beneath their icy crusts.
